quality.rationale: 完整弗里德曼方程推导结合线性扰动稳定性分析，具明确历史宇宙学物理背景与可替换物源场景

origin.question:
Problem 19.31. A universe is isotropic, homogeneous, and contains only pressureless dust and "vacuum polarization" stress energy, so that $\mathbf{T}_{\mu 
u} = ho_0 \mathfrak{u}_\mu \mathfrak{u}_
u -  rac{\Lambda}{8\pi} \mathfrak{g}_{\mu 
u}$ where $\mathfrak{u}^\mu$ is the 4-velocity field of the matter. Show that there is a static solution for the metric, but that it is unstable. (This cosmology is called the "Einstein universe".)
origin.answer: Static solution exists with spatial curvature $k=+1$, scale factor $R=\Lambda^{-1/2}$, and dust density $\rho_0=\Lambda/(4\pi)$. The universe is unstable because linear perturbations obey $\delta \ddot{R} - \Lambda \delta R = 0$, leading to exponential growth.
origin.solution: ## SolutionSolution 19.31. The complete set of equations for isotropic, homogeneous universes is (see Problems 19.13 or 19.14)$$\left(\frac {\dot {\mathrm {R}}}{\dot {\mathrm {R}}}\right)^{2} = \frac {- \mathrm {k}}{\mathrm {R}^{2}} + \frac {8 \pi \rho}{3} \tag {1a}$$$$2 \ddot {\frac {R}{R}} = - \left(\dot {\frac {R}{R}}\right)^{2} - \frac {k}{R^{2}} - 8 \pi p. \tag {1b}$$Since the universe contains only dust stress-energy, $\rho_0$ , and "vacuum polarization" stress-energy $\Lambda$ , we have$$\rho = \rho_{0} + \Lambda / 8 \pi \tag {2a}$$$$p = - \Lambda / 8 \pi . \tag {2b}$$Since all time derivatives of $\mathbf{R}$ higher than the second may be expressed as linear combinations of $\dot{\mathbf{R}}$ and $\ddot{\mathbf{R}}$ by Equations (1), the necessary and sufficient condition for a static solution is$$\dot {\mathbf {R}} = \ddot {\mathbf {R}} = 0. \tag {3}$$With the conditions of Equations (3) and (2), Equations (1) become$$\mathrm {k} / \mathrm {R}^{2} = (8 \pi \rho_{0} + \Lambda) / 3 \tag {4a}$$$$\mathrm {k} / \mathrm {R}^{2} = \Lambda \tag {4b}$$which imply$$\rho_{0} = \Lambda / 4 \pi \tag {5a}$$$$\mathbf {R} = \Lambda^{- \frac {1}{2}}. \tag {5b}$$Since $\rho_0 > 0$ , Equation (4b) now implies $k = +1$ . To investigate the stability of the "Einstein universe," first combine Equations (1) togive$$\frac {2 \ddot {\mathrm {R}}}{\mathrm {R}} = - \frac {8 \pi}{3} \rho - 8 \pi \mathrm {p} = - \frac {8 \pi}{3} \rho_{0} + \frac {2}{3} \Lambda \tag {6}$$and let$$\mathbf {R} = \boldsymbol {\Lambda}^{- \frac {1}{2}} + \delta \mathbf {R} \tag {7a}$$$$\rho_{0} = \frac {\Lambda}{4 \pi} + \delta \rho_{0} \tag {7b}$$Equation (6), to order $\delta \mathbf{R}$ , now becomes$$2 \Lambda^{\frac {1}{2}} (\delta \ddot {\mathbf {R}}) = - \frac {8 \pi}{3} \delta \rho_{0}. \tag {8}$$Now, since the total amount of dust is conserved, the dust stress-energy obeys$$\rho_{0} \mathbf {R}^{3} = \text {c o n s t a n t}$$$$\frac {\delta \rho_{0}}{\rho_{0}} = - 3 \frac {\delta R}{R}. \tag {9}$$Equation (8) then gives$$\left(\delta \ddot {\mathbf {R}}\right) - \Lambda \delta \mathbf {R} = 0, \tag {10}$$i.e. the perturbation $\delta R$ grows exponentially in time. Hence, the universe is unstable.
